title: Teeth Steroid Extraction for "Steroid profiling in human primary teeth via liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry for long-term retrospective steroid measurement"
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4up6n","text":"Steroid hormones are important modulators of many physiological processes, and","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1eocv","text":"measurements of steroids in blood, saliva, and urine matrices are widely used to","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"q119","text":"assess endocrine pathologies and stress. However, these matrices cannot be used to","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4i4ki","text":"retrospectively assess early-life stress and developmental endocrine pathologies,","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fk0bc","text":"because they do not integrate steroid levels over the long term. A novel biological","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"68tp3","text":"matrix in which to measure steroids is primary teeth (or “baby teeth”). Primary teeth","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2c2ca","text":"develop early in life and accumulate various endogenous molecules during their","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9c2l0","text":"gradual formation. Here, we developed and validated the first assay to measure","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"lp5p","text":"steroids in human primary teeth using liquid chromatography-tandem spectrometry","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"67mn1","text":"(LC-MS/MS). Our assay is highly sensitive, specific, accurate, and precise. It allows for","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fvdgn","text":"the simultaneous quantification of 17 steroids in primary teeth (16 of which have not","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f41vs","text":"been examined previously in primary teeth). Overall, steroid levels in primary teeth","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3bpg0","text":"were relatively low, and 8 steroids were quantifiable. Levels of","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"72f5v","text":"dehydroepiandrosterone, cortisol, and progesterone were the highest of the 17 steroids","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dnb58","text":"examined. Next, we used this assay to perform steroid profiling in primary teeth from","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ajilk","text":"males and females. The same 8 steroids were quantifiable, and no sex differences","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1s9rv","text":"were found. Levels of androgens (androstenedione and testosterone) were positively","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b0a8l","text":"correlated, and levels of glucocorticoids (cortisol, cortisone, corticosterone, 11-","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e677c","text":"dehydrocorticosterone) were also positively correlated. These data demonstrate that","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7lf3o","text":"multiple steroids can be quantified by LC-MS/MS in human primary teeth, and this","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ovjo","text":"method potentially provides a powerful new way to retrospectively assess early-life","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bdsm3","text":"stress and developmental endocrine pathologies.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Put 5 1.4mm beads in a 2mL Bead ruptor tube
Label 0.6mL polypropelene microcentrifuge tubes tocorrespond with bead ruptor tubes
Label LC-MS/MS vials to corresond with bead ruptor tubes andput glass inserts in vials
Make enough 1X I.S. to use for this asay

Rinse all 12x75mm Fisher culture tubes needed for experimentwith 1mL MeOH, vortex for 2sec, dump MeOH waste into waste beaker, add another1mL MeOH to each tube, vortex for 2 sec, dump MeOH into waste and placeinverted tubes in tube rack in drying oven at 60C
Label rinsed and dried culture tubes
Cover all rinsed, dried and labelled tubes (right-side up)with aluminum foil ensuring complete covering of tubes
Wash all teeth to remove saliva and blood
Remove all reagents from 4C or -20C and allow to come toroom temp
Retrieve the cleaned pieces of three teeth from the drawerand record the weight
Crush each piece into powder with the tooth crusher
Put all tooth powder into corresponding bead ruptor tube
Add 10ul STD to appropriate bead ruptor tubes
Add 1mL room temp HPLC ACN to all tubes
Add 50ul I.S. to all tubes excpet BLKBLK
Remove 1000ul supernatant (94%) and put in 12x75mm culturetube
Remove hexane and put in waste
Transfer all supernatant to 0.6mL centrifuge tube
Transfer 50ul supernatant to LC insert using gel loadingtips
creator: Ruolan S. Wu
